meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Claude Code Setup to Overcome Project Paralysis
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with no project ideas. Learn to use Claude Code and Antigravity to generate, build, and deploy your first real-world AI application today.
content.answer_block: The 'no project idea' trap is solved by offloading ideation to Claude Code while using Antigravity to scaffold production-ready architecture. By treating your IDE as a collaborative pair programmer, you transform abstract concepts into executable code, allowing you to bypass the blank-page syndrome and focus on shipping high-impact features.
content.intro:
The blinking cursor on an empty code editor is the silent killer of potential. You have the ambition and the tools, yet the chasm between 'learning to code' and 'building a product' feels insurmountable. You aren't lacking talent; you are lacking a systematic workflow to bridge the gap between intent and execution.

Today, we dismantle that barrier. By integrating Claude Code into your local environment and leveraging Antigravity for architectural blueprints, you will stop waiting for inspiration and start engineering it. This isn't just about writing code; it's about mastering the AI-augmented development lifecycle that separates modern senior engineers from the rest.

steps.title: Configure your AI-native workspace
steps.description: Establish a consistent environment where Claude Code can access your file system and execute commands securely.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Architect. Define a directory structure for a modern AI-powered SaaS starter kit that follows clean architecture principles.

steps.title: Generate a high-impact project roadmap
steps.description: Use Claude to brainstorm a project that fits your skill level but demonstrates advanced technical proficiency.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate 3 SaaS project ideas for a student portfolio that solve a real-world pain point and require integration with at least one external API.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Propose a project roadmap for a SaaS that tracks personal carbon footprint using public climate data APIs.'

steps.title: Scaffold the technical foundation
steps.description: Transform the architectural plan into a working codebase with standardized boilerplate.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a detailed dependency graph and setup script for a React and Node.js application that adheres to the folder structure defined in step 1.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize project with Vite, Tailwind CSS, and Express server boilerplate.'

steps.title: Implement core business logic
steps.description: Iteratively build features by providing Claude with specific business rules and expected data flows.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust function to fetch and normalize data from the selected API, including error handling and type definitions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Implement a fetch utility with Zod schema validation for the API response.'

steps.title: Refactor and document for production
steps.description: Clean up your code and ensure it is documented well enough for a technical recruiter to review.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the current codebase for security vulnerabilities and performance bottlenecks, then generate a comprehensive README.md.

pro_tips: Treat Claude Code as a junior developer: review every suggestion for 'hallucinated' imports or deprecated dependencies.
Always commit your code before triggering a major Claude Code refactor to maintain a clean git history.
Use Antigravity to generate unit tests before writing the implementation to enforce TDD (Test Driven Development) practices.
Keep your context window lean by clearing out irrelevant temporary files before asking Claude to analyze the entire codebase.
Use the 'claude ask' command to explain complex logic in your code to yourself; if you can't explain it back, the code is too complex.
resources: Modern Web Development Best Practices: https://developer.mozilla.org
content.next_steps: Once your first prototype is functional, focus on deploying it to Vercel or Railway and adding a 'Live Demo' link to your GitHub profile.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the workflow, and the roadmap. The only variable remaining is your commitment to shipping. Stop waiting for the perfect moment and start building the future today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
